description: In a brightly lit living room, Monica sits in an armchair. An unidentified off-screen speaker says, "ADCAP gal."
Monica continues to smile.

Ross stands up from the sofa and steps toward Monica, gesturing with both hands. "Try to imagine this. The phone bill arrives, but you don't pay it right away. Why not?" he asks. Audience laughter is heard.
Monica tilts her head slightly, keeping her gaze on Ross.
Ross smiles and points at Monica, saying, "Because you're a kook." Audience laughter is heard.
Monica looks up and away. Audience laughter continues.
Ross lowers his arm and steps back slightly. "Instead, you wait until they send you a notice," he says.
Monica smiles and leans forward in her chair.
Monica says, "I could do that," as audience laughter is heard.
Rachel stands up from the coffee table and steps forward, pointing at Monica. "Okay, okay. Then you let me go grocery shopping," Rachel says.
Monica points back at Rachel and says, "No problem." Rachel raises one hand and says, "I am not done yet."
Monica lowers her hand and looks at Rachel.
Rachel points her finger while speaking, "And I buy laundry detergent. But it's not the one with the easy pour spout."
Monica raises both hands, palms up, and says, "Why would someone do that?" Audience laughter is heard.
Ross looks down, smiling. Rachel rests her chin on her hands.
Ross says, "One might wonder." Monica looks at him, her hands still raised.
Monica lowers her hands, smiling.
Monica touches her hair and says, "But I would be fine with that," as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler walks from the background toward the group, gesturing with one hand. "Someone's left a glass on the coffee table. There's no coaster. It's" he says.
Chandler stands near the television, gesturing as he speaks. "a cold drink. It's a hot day. Little beads of condensation are inching their way closer and closer to the surface of the wood."
Monica waves her hands rapidly in front of her chest and says loudly, "Stop it." Audience laughter is heard.
Monica lowers her hands, breathing visibly, while the group watches her. Audience laughter continues.
Monica places a hand on her chest and says, "Oh, my God."
Monica gestures with both hands and says, "It's true. Who am I?"
Ross looks at Monica and says, "Monica, your mom." Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe raises her hand, making a repeated downward stabbing motion, while emitting high-pitched rhythmic vocalizations. Audience laughter is heard.
The group looks at Phoebe. Joey enters through the front door holding a cordless phone to his ear and walks into the living room.
Joey stops walking, holding the phone, and says, "Oh, my God."
Joey raises his free hand while Ross stands near the refrigerator.
Joey says, "Okay," while lowering his free hand.
Ross looks at Joey and takes a drink from a bottle.
Joey says, "I'll be there."
Joey lowers the phone antenna, clicks a button, and tosses the phone onto the sofa. Ross lowers his bottle.
Joey looks toward the group and says, "That was my agent." Audience laughter is heard.
The group looks at Joey. Joey turns his body to face them fully, raising one hand.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "My agent has just gotten me a job in the new Al Pacino movie."
The group members stand up quickly, raising their hands and talking loudly over one another. Audience laughter is heard.
Joey gestures with both arms spread wide and says, "Can you believe this? Al Pacino. This guy's the reason I became an actor."
Joey points his finger forward and says loudly, "I'm out of order. You're out of order."
Joey waves his arms and shouts, "This whole courtroom's out of order." Audience laughter is heard.
Joey shakes his fists, smiling. Ross takes a sip from his bottle.
Chandler says, "Seriously, what's the part?" Joey gestures with his hands and says in a low voice, "Just when I thought I was out, they pull me back in."
Audience laughter is heard as Ross smiles.
Ross says, "Seriously? Joey. What's the" before pausing.
Joey lowers his gaze and rubs his mouth. Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel looks at Joey and says, "what?"
Joey puts his hands on his hips and looks down.
Joey looks up and says, "I'm his butt double, okay?" Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel and Monica look at each other, pressing their lips together. Chandler keeps his lips pressed together.
Joey gestures with one hand and says, "I play Al Pacino's butt." Audience laughter continues.
The group remains silent, looking at Joey.
Joey raises his hand and says, "All right?"
Joey gestures toward the kitchen, then points to himself. "He goes into the shower, and then I'm his butt," he says. Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel and Monica cover their mouths.
Rachel keeps her hands over her mouth and says, "Oh, my God," while laughing.
Joey spreads his arms and says, "Come on, you guys. This is a real movie and Al Pacino's in it, and that's big."
Chandler gestures with his hand and says, "Oh, no, it's terrific."
Chandler points at Joey and says, "You deserve this. After all your years of struggling you've finally been able to crack your way into" Audience laughter interrupts him.
Chandler stops speaking and looks at Joey. Joey stares back, his expression unchanged. Audience laughter continues.
Joey waves his hand and says, "okay, fine. Make jokes. I don't care. This is a big break for me."
Ross nods and says, "No, you're right. You're right. It is. So you're going to invite us"
Ross smiles and says, "all to the big opening?"
Joey stares at Ross, turns, and walks toward the hallway. Audience laughter rises in volume.
The scene changes to an exterior view of a brick apartment building with trees in the foreground.
Now inside a hallway, Monica, wearing a red robe, inserts a key into an apartment door. She opens it to reveal Joey in a striped robe.
Joey hands Monica a folded newspaper and says, "I need to borrow some moisturizer." Monica takes the newspaper and asks, "For what?"
Joey looks at Monica, shifting his posture. Audience laughter is heard.
Joey says, "Today's the big day," looking at Monica.
Monica looks at Joey, holding the newspaper. Audience laughter is heard.
In a kitchen, Monica stands near the open front door holding a newspaper. Joey stands in the doorway wearing a striped robe.
Joey points to his chin and says, "and you got a Tweezers. I think I have a rogue hair."
Joey steps past Monica into the apartment.
Monica points toward the hallway and says, "God. Okay, go into the bathroom. Use whatever you want."
As Joey walks down the hallway, Monica calls out, "Just don't ever tell me what you did in there." Joey replies, "Thank you." Monica closes the front door.
Chandler enters the kitchen from the hallway, holding a cordless phone.

Chandler asks, "Where's Joey's? Mom's in the room." Monica replies, "He's in the bathroom. I don't think you want to go in there." Chandler says, "Oh, come on. We're roommates," and walks to the bathroom door, grabbing the handle.
Chandler opens the bathroom door, immediately covers his eyes with one hand, and screams loudly.
Chandler stumbles backward into the living room, hunching over. Audience laughter is audible. Rachel enters the living room from the hallway.
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "I warned you."
Rachel asks, "Who is being loud?" Chandler stands upright, points at Monica, and says, "Oh, that would be Monica."
Chandler walks to the refrigerator and says, "Hey, listen, can I borrow a couple of things? Aurora spent the night. I really want to make her breakfast." Monica says, "Oh, you get"
Monica finishes, "the whole night." Chandler opens the refrigerator and says, "Yeah, well, I only have 20 minutes until Ethan." Rachel removes a milk carton from the fridge.
Chandler pulls items from the refrigerator and loads them into his arms.
Holding the items, Chandler looks forward and asks, "Do I sense a little bit of resentment?"
Chandler shakes his head slightly and says, "No resentment."
Chandler continues organizing the items in his arms and says, "Believe me, it's worth it."
Chandler says, "Okay. You know how in a relationship you have these key moments..." gesturing with one hand.
Chandler continues, "...that you know you'll remember for the rest of your life? Well, every"
Chandler adds a carton to his stack and says, "single second is like that with Aurora."
Chandler turns toward the hallway.
Chandler says, "And I've just wasted about 35 of them talking to you people."
Chandler turns back and asks, "So, Monica, can you help me with the door?" Monica replies, "Sure," and steps toward the front door.
Monica opens the door, and Chandler walks past her into the hallway.
Monica stands in the doorway, gesturing with her hands as she says, "Chandler. The old Monica would remind you to scrub..."
Monica continues, "...that Teflon pan with a plastic brush."
Monica lowers her hands and smiles.
Monica shakes her head, laughing slightly, and says, "I'm not going to do that." She closes the door.
The setting changes to a daytime aerial view of the New York City skyline.
The setting changes to a film set filled with equipment and crew members. A prop bathtub with a showerhead is visible.
Joey, wearing a dark blue robe, gestures to a crew member and says, "Excuse me. Can you see if they can warm it up in here?"
Joey adds, "I'm a little concerned about Goosebumps." A director seated in a chair calls out, "Okay, everybody ready?" Joey steps into the prop shower.
Joey speaks to the director from the shower, "Listen, I just want to thank you for this great opportunity."
Joey gestures with his hands and continues, "I mean, listen, I know this is just the first step but I"
Joey says, "hope that someday" before the director interrupts, speaking into a handheld device, "lose the robe."
Joey nods and replies, "Okay, losing the robe."
Joey turns and begins pulling the robe off his shoulders.
Joey tosses the robe out of the shower, standing bare-chested. He says, "Okay, and the robe is lost." The director says, "Okay, everybody would like to get this in one take, please."
The director says, "Let's roll it." A crew member adds, "Water's"
The crew member finishes, "working," as water begins spraying from the showerhead.
Joey flinches slightly as the water hits him.
The director says, "And action." Joey leans back into the water.
Joey rubs his arm and chest, clenching his jaw as he stands under the spray.
The director calls out, "And cut."
Joey stops moving and opens his eyes.
The director looks at Joey and asks, "Hey, butt guy. What the hell are you doing?"
Joey looks back at the director and shrugs slightly, holding his hands out.
The director says, "No, that was clenching." Joey looks surprised.
Joey steps forward slightly, lowering his hands.
Joey gestures with both hands and says, "The way I see it, the guy's upset here, you know?"
Joey continues, "I mean, his wife's dead, his brother's missing. I think his"
Joey finishes, "butt would be angry here," pointing down briefly.
The director looks at Joey without moving.
The director speaks flatly into his device, "I think his butt would like to get the shot before lunch."
Joey smiles and nods.
The director says, "Once again, rolling. Water working."
Joey steps backward under the falling water.
The director calls, "And action."
Joey closes his eyes and clenches his fists tightly, holding his arms stiffly at his sides.
The director says, "And cut. What was that?" Joey opens his eyes and raises his hands.
Joey replies, "I was going for quiet desperation,"
Joey hugs his arms around his chest and shivers.
Joey finishes, "if you have to ask."
The setting changes to the exterior of a brick building with a red-framed storefront awning. A yellow taxi drives past.
The setting changes to a bedroom. Chandler and Aurora lie side-by-side in bed, holding hands above the covers.
Looking at their joined hands, Chandler says, "God, I love these fingers."
Aurora smiles and says, "Thank you."
Chandler pulls his hand away, points to his own fingers, and says, "No, actually, I meant my fingers." Aurora laughs.
Chandler wiggles his fingers in the air.
Chandler says, "Look at them. Look at how happy they are."
Aurora pulls Chandler's hand down, and they kiss briefly. She then turns her head to look at her wristwatch.
Aurora sits up quickly and says, "Oh, my God, I'm late." Chandler grasps her arm and says, "Oh, no, don't go. Don't go."
Chandler maintains his grip on her arm as they look at each other.
Chandler says softly, "Don't go."
Aurora pulls her arm away and gets out of bed. Chandler sits up, holding the blanket to his chest.
Chandler watches her and says, "Look at that. She's leaving."
Aurora picks up a floral dress and says, "I'm sorry. He'll be waiting for me."
Chandler says, "I thought you talked to Rick."
Aurora pulls the dress over her head and replies, "It's not rick." Chandler says, "Ethan, he spent the whole day with you."
Aurora adjusts the dress and says, "No, it's Andrew."
Aurora finishes pulling the dress down. Chandler remains seated in bed, watching her.
In a bedroom, Chandler stands in a white t-shirt. A woman with curly hair, Aurora, stands across from him. Chandler exhales.
Chandler says, "I know there'll be many moments in the years to come when I'll regret asking the following question," looking down briefly.
Chandler stands still, maintaining eye contact with Aurora.
Chandler asks, "And Andrew is"
Aurora looks at him, holding a strand of her hair.
Aurora twirls her hair and says, "new."
Chandler nods slightly.
Chandler says, "So what you're saying is you're not completely fulfilled by Rick, Ethan and myself."
Aurora shakes her head and replies, "No, not exactly." Chandler asks, "What?"
Chandler gestures and says, "You know, most women would kill for three guys like us." Aurora adjusts a ring on her finger. A laugh track plays.
Aurora continues adjusting her ring, looking down before meeting his gaze.
Aurora asks, "What do you want?"
Chandler looks at her steadily.
Chandler replies, "You." Aurora opens her hands and says, "You have me."
Chandler points and says, "No, just you." Aurora touches her earring, asking, "What do you mean?" Chandler leans slightly forward and says, "Lose the other guys."
Aurora points at herself.
Aurora gestures with her hands and asks, "Like, all of them?" Chandler looks back at her. A laugh track is audible.
Chandler steps forward slightly.
Chandler gestures with one hand, "We're great together."
Chandler asks, "Why not?" Aurora steps closer.
Aurora, speaking with rising volume, says, "Why can't we just have what we have now? Why can't we just talk and laugh and make love without feeling obligated to"
Aurora finishes, "one another?"
Chandler looks at her with a neutral expression.
Chandler replies, "And up until tonight, I thought that's what you wanted"
Aurora looks down. Chandler moves to sit on the bed.
Chandler sits, and Aurora sits beside him. He says, "know."
Chandler gestures with his hands, saying, "Part of me wants that, but it's like I'm two know."
Chandler continues, "I mean, one guy's saying, Shut up. This is great."
Chandler lowers his hands, looking at Aurora.
Chandler continues, gesturing with both hands, "But there's this other guy."
Chandler says, "Actually, it's the guy who wells up every time the Grinch's heart grows three sizes and breaks that measuring"
Chandler finishes, "device." Aurora looks at him.
Chandler pauses, looking at Aurora.
Chandler points outward with both thumbs, "He's saying, you know, this is too hard. Get out. Get out."
Chandler places a hand over his mouth. Aurora looks at him.
Aurora asks, "So which one of the two guys will you listen to?"
Chandler removes his hand from his mouth and says, "I don't know. I have to listen to both of them. They don't exactly let each other finish."
Aurora and Chandler maintain eye contact in silence.
Chandler continues looking back at her, his hands resting on his legs.
Chandler says softly, "The second guy."
Aurora looks down, stands up, and picks up her jacket.
Aurora says, "Well,"
Aurora turns and walks toward the door, holding her jacket.
Aurora stops, turns back to Chandler, and says, "tell me if you change your mind."
Chandler stands up and approaches Aurora.
They embrace and kiss, with Chandler holding the sides of her face.
Chandler pulls back slightly and says, "It's"
Aurora looks at him, holding her jacket.
Chandler says, "sorry. The first guy runs the lips." A laugh track is audible.
Aurora smiles slightly, turns away, and walks out the door. Chandler places his hands on his hips.
Chandler covers his face with both hands, turns, and falls backward onto the bed. A laugh track plays.
Outside a red-awning building at night, people stand on the sidewalk. The setting changes to an apartment living room where Chandler and Ross sit on a couch.
Ross points at Chandler and says, "Look at it this way."
Ross points his finger back and forth.
Ross says, "You dumped her,"
Phoebe, Rachel, and Monica sit around the living room, listening.
Ross gestures with his hands, saying, "right? I mean, this woman was unbelievably sexy"
Ross continues, "and beautiful, intelligent, unattainable."
Ross rests his hands in his lap, looking at Chandler.
Ross asks, "Tell me why you did this." A laugh track is audible.
Joey walks through the purple door wearing a black turtleneck. The group greets him audibly.
Monica, wearing a red turtleneck, points at Joey and says, "Hey, wait a minute."
Monica continues, "Aren't you the guy who plays the butt in the new Al Pacino movie?" Joey walks toward a chair.
Joey sits in the chair, resting his arms on the sides and shaking his head slightly.
Joey says softly, "No." Ross leans forward and asks, "What happened, big guy?"
Ross continues looking at Joey.
Chandler looks at Ross and asks, "Big guy?"
Ross gestures defensively with his hands.
Ross replies, "It felt like a big guy moment." A laugh track plays.
Joey sits looking forward.
Joey says, "I got fired." Monica exclaims, and Joey continues, "Oh, yeah. They said I acted too much with it." A laugh track is audible.
Joey gestures slightly with his right hand and drops his arms.

In a living room, Joey sits in an armchair. Phoebe and Rachel sit on a sofa beside him. Joey gestures with both hands and says, "I told everybody about this. Now everyone's going to go to the theater expecting to see me."
Rachel leans forward, resting her hand near Joey's knee, and says, "Joe, you know what? No one is going to be able to" Joey looks at her.
Rachel finishes, "...tell." Joey looks straight ahead and replies, "My mom will." A laugh track plays.
Chandler and Ross sit on another sofa nearby. Ross clasps his hands in his lap.
Ross says, "Something so sweet and disturbing about that." A laugh track plays.
Joey looks down and rubs his nose, then looks up again.
Joey raises his hands, palms open, and says, "I've done nothing but crappy plays for six years and I finally get my shot and I blow it."
Monica, sitting at a nearby table with her hands clasped, says, "Wait a minute."
Monica continues, "Maybe this wasn't your shot."
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Yeah, I think when it's your shot, you know it's your shot."
Ross keeps his hands clasped.
Ross brings his clasped hands to his chin and asks, "Did it feel like your shot?"
Joey looks forward with a neutral expression.
Joey gestures slightly with his right hand and replies, "Hard to tell. I was naked." A laugh track plays.
Phoebe turns toward Joey.
Phoebe, shaking her head, says, "I don't think this was your shot. I don't even think you just get one shot."
Phoebe gestures with her hand as she continues, "I really believe big things are going to happen for you. I do."
Phoebe moves her hands in a wide gesture and says, "And you've got to just keep thinking about the day that some kid is going to run up to his friends and go, I got the part. I got the..."
Phoebe finishes, "...part. I'm going to be Joey Tribiani's ass." A laugh track plays.
Monica, Chandler, and Ross smile. Joey's mouth forms a slight smile.
Joey says, "So nice," and leans over toward Phoebe.
Joey and Phoebe embrace. Chandler and Ross look at each other and open their arms.
Chandler and Ross embrace tightly while a laugh track plays.
Monica stands up from the table and says, "I'm sorry, Joey. I'm going to go to bed, guys." She walks toward the hallway.
Chandler points toward the floor and asks, "Mon, you're going to leave your shoes out here?" Monica stops and turns back.
Monica looks down at her white shoes on the floor, then looks up and says, "Uh-huh," with a small smile.
Rachel leans forward and asks, "Really?"
Rachel gestures with one hand and asks, "Just casually strewn about in that reckless, haphazard manner?"
Monica looks at Rachel.
Monica waves her hand dismissively and says, "Doesn't matter. I'll get up tomorrow or not."
Monica drops her arms and smiles broadly.
Monica adds, "Whenever," and turns to leave.
Monica walks down the hallway. Ross watches her go, then turns to Chandler.
Ross nods and says, "She is a kook." A laugh track plays.
The group sits in the living room as Monica's white shoes remain on the floor.
The scene changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night.
The setting changes to a bedroom. Monica lies in bed, staring up at the ceiling.
Monica keeps her gaze fixed upward and says, "If it bothers you that much, just go out and get the shoes."
Monica blinks, remaining still in bed.
Monica shakes her head slightly on the pillow and says, "No, don't do this. This is stupid. I don't have to prove anything. I'm going to go get them,"
Monica grips the top edge of her blanket.
Monica pulls the blanket up tightly to her chin and says, "But then everyone will know."
Monica lies still, staring at the ceiling.
Monica says, "Unless I get them and then wake up really early and put them back." She pauses, then adds, "I need help," her voice trailing off as she pulls a pillow over her head.
The setting changes back to the living room. The friends sit around the coffee table as Rachel reaches into a bowl.
Phoebe looks at a small item in her hands and sings, "All day."

Phoebe sings, "Dingo." Joey tilts his head back and groans, "Oh, no, not that." Phoebe continues singing, "All you envies are shake. Come on," as the others begin snapping their fingers in rhythm. Credits text appears on screen.
The friends stand up from the sofas, snapping their fingers and moving to the beat of the song. Credits continue to appear on screen.
The group sings, "Tinker," while snapping their fingers and swaying.
The group continues to sing and sway together in the living room.
The group sings in harmony, "Simply let hang. All you want is a dinkle." They continue snapping and swaying as more credits roll on screen.
The friends sing together, spreading their arms out and gesturing in synchronization. Credits continue displaying on screen.
Chandler and Ross point at each other and sing loudly. Joey dances beside them, smiling.
The group finishes their song and laughs. The screen turns black.
The screen remains black.
